question: Will Jean-Philippe Mateta record the most assists in the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League?
description:
This market will resolve to the player who records the most assists through all main tournament rounds of the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League competition.
		
In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by UEFA Europa Conference League rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025-26 Europa Conference League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the UEFA Europa Conference League most assists market clusters tightly around 44% for leaders Carlo Holse, Orbelín Pineda, Martial Godo, Lee Jae-Sung, Lazaros Rota, and Jean-Philippe Mateta, reflecting their shared tally of three assists each after four to five league phase matches. This parity stems from balanced chance creation across teams like AEK Athens, Celtic, PAOK, and Standard Liège, where wingers and attacking midfielders have thrived amid high-possession styles and set-piece prowess. Recent outings, including Pineda's key pass in AEK's midweek victory and Godo's crossing threat for Celtic, sustained the deadlock without a standout surge. With one league phase game remaining plus potential playoffs, traders weigh similar fixture difficulties and rotation risks, keeping the race fluid and unpredictable.

This market will resolve to the player who records the most yellow cards through all main tournament rounds of the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League competition.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by UEFA Europa Conference League rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The UEFA Europa Conference League most yellow cards market remains razor-tight among midfield enforcers Cher Ndour, Darko Hrka, and Petros Mantalos, each hovering around 45% implied probability after accumulating 4-5 bookings through the league phase's opening fixtures. Trader consensus reflects their foul-prone styles—Ndour's tenacious Benfica pressing, Hrka's rugged Rijeka challenges, and Mantalos' combative AEK Athens duels—yielding similar disciplinary tallies despite varying minutes played. Recent developments, including Hrka and Mantalos dodging suspensions in high-stakes clashes, keep the race bunched, while fixture congestion for their sides heightens tackle risks ahead. Lower-tier options like Páez trail due to lighter exposure, underscoring the wisdom of crowds in pricing parity among proven card magnets.

question: Ethereum Up or Down - February 25, 4:10PM-4:15PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Ethereum price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the ETH/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/eth-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream ETH/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Tony Gonzalez out as US Rep by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Tony Gonzalez ceases to be the U.S Representative for Texas' 23rd congressional district for any period of time between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Gonzalez's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Tony Gonzalez and the US federal government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Tony Gonzalez out as US Rep by March 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Tony Gonzalez ceases to be the U.S Representative for Texas' 23rd congressional district for any period of time between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Gonzalez's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Tony Gonzalez and the US federal government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rep. Tony Gonzales' outright win in the March 5 Texas 23rd District Republican primary, capturing 51% against challenger Brandon Herrera's 27%, drives the 90.5% "No" odds on him departing his US House seat by March 31. Despite 2023 censure by the Texas GOP over support for bipartisan infrastructure, gun safety measures, and same-sex marriage protections, Gonzales avoided a runoff and reaffirmed his commitment to serve through the November general election. No resignation announcements, expulsion efforts, or legal challenges have surfaced, reinforcing trader consensus on short-term seat stability amid typical House member retention rates. Upcoming general election polling could influence longer-term markets.

question: Will Stripe acquire Paypal in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if it is officially announced that Paypal will be, has been, or is being acquired by or merged with Stripe by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying acquisition or acquisition announcement must include the acquisition of a controlling interest in Paypal by Stripe. A "controlling interest" is defined as an ownership stake sufficient to control the company's strategic decisions, typically more than 50% of equity, or equivalent control via voting rights, governance rights, board control, or other mechanisms. Transactions or investments that do not result in a transfer of controlling interest, such as minority stake purchases, will not count.

An announcement of a qualifying acquisition or merger by Paypal or Paypal and Stripe will qualify for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the announced acquisition/merger actually occurs.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Paypal and Stripe, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will Stripe acquire Paypal in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if it is officially announced that Paypal will be, has been, or is being acquired by or merged with Stripe by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying acquisition or acquisition announcement must include the acquisition of a controlling interest in Paypal by Stripe. A "controlling interest" is defined as an ownership stake sufficient to control the company's strategic decisions, typically more than 50% of equity, or equivalent control via voting rights, governance rights, board control, or other mechanisms. Transactions or investments that do not result in a transfer of controlling interest, such as minority stake purchases, will not count.

An announcement of a qualifying acquisition or merger by Paypal or Paypal and Stripe will qualify for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the announced acquisition/merger actually occurs.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Paypal and Stripe, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 83% market-implied probability favoring "No" on a Stripe-PayPal acquisition in 2026 stems primarily from stark valuation mismatches and antitrust barriers, as Stripe's private valuation hovers around $65 billion post-2024 tender offer while PayPal's public market cap exceeds $70 billion amid stagnant growth. No official statements, leaks, or strategic signals from either firm suggest merger interest—Stripe prioritizes developer tools and enterprise payments, diverging from PayPal's consumer focus. Recent PayPal earnings misses and Stripe's subdued funding rounds reinforce trader consensus that regulatory scrutiny from FTC/DOJ would torpedo any deal, with no catalysts like bidding wars or IPOs on the horizon to shift odds.

question: Will Nikolas Veratschnig record the most red cards in the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League?
description:
This market will resolve to the player who records the most red cards through all main tournament rounds of the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League competition.

For the purpose of this market, both direct red cards, or red cards that result from the accumulation of two yellow cards in the same match will be considered in the final count.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by UEFA Europa Conference League rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025-26 Europa Conference League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Joseph Mbong leads trader consensus for most red cards in the UEFA Europa Conference League at 58% implied probability, anchored by his straight red in FC Santa Coloma's league phase opener against Celje, positioning him ahead in a tournament where repeat dismissals are uncommon. Alexandre Penetra (Boavista), Nikolas Veratschnig (LASK), Pep Chavarría (Legia Warsaw), and Borna Sosa (Ajax) cluster around 39-40%, reflecting their combative profiles—Penetra's recent domestic yellow accumulation, Veratschnig's physical midfield battles, Chavarría's fiery wing play, and Sosa's history of ejections in top leagues. No new reds this week, but their teams' grueling schedules and tactical aggression sustain elevated risk as the phase progresses.

If the 2025-26 Europa Conference League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Joseph Mbong leads trader consensus for most red cards in the UEFA Europa Conference League at 58% implied probability, anchored by his straight red in FC Santa Coloma's league phase opener against Celje, positioning him ahead in a tournament where repeat dismissals are uncommon. Alexandre Penetra (Boavista), Nikolas Veratschnig (LASK), Pep Chavarría (Legia Warsaw), and Borna Sosa (Ajax) cluster around 39-40%, reflecting their combative profiles—Penetra's recent domestic yellow accumulation, Veratschnig's physical midfield battles, Chavarría's fiery wing play, and Sosa's history of ejections in top leagues. No new reds this week, but their teams' grueling schedules and tactical aggression sustain elevated risk as the phase progresses.

question: Will Stripe acquire any part of Paypal in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if it is officially announced that any part of PayPal will be, has been, or is being acquired by Stripe, or that PayPal is being merged with Stripe, by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Acquiring a part of PayPal refers to any material acquisition of a subset of PayPal by Stripe, including but not limited to a PayPal subsidiary, business unit, or equity interest. A total acquisition of PayPal by Stripe will count. Business partnerships between PayPal and Stripe will not count.

An announcement of a qualifying acquisition or merger by PayPal or PayPal and Stripe will qualify for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the announced acquisition/merger actually occurs.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from PayPal and Stripe, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will Stripe acquire any part of Paypal in 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket pegs the odds of Stripe acquiring any part of PayPal in 2026 at 52% yes, driven primarily by PayPal's undervalued public market cap around $70 billion—trading at a discounted 8x EV/EBITDA amid slowing transaction growth and competitive erosion from fintech rivals like Stripe. Stripe's robust $65 billion private valuation and aggressive expansion fuel acquisition speculation, though antitrust scrutiny and Stripe's funding needs create the razor-thin balance. Key tipping points include PayPal's Q4 earnings in late January 2025, which could reveal further weakness or resilience, alongside potential Stripe IPO filings or Fed rate cuts spurring M&A activity in a loosening financial environment.

question: Will Nikolas Veratschnig record the most cards in the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League?
description:
This market will resolve to the player who records the most disciplinary cards through all main tournament rounds of the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League competition.

For the purpose of this market, a disciplinary card will count as a yellow card, a direct red card, or a red card resulting from the accumulation of two yellow cards in the same match. A 2nd yellow card accumulated in a match which results in a red card will count as one card in the final count, not a separate yellow and red card. The tally of these cards will be counted as reflected on the official UEFA Europa Conference League website (https://www.uefa.com/uefaconferenceleague/statistics/players/disciplinary/?sortBy=yellow_cards&order=desc).

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by UEFA Europa Conference League rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025-26 Europa Conference League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

events.title: Epstein storage units raided in 2026?
events.description:
Recent reports show that Jeffrey Epstein previously used multiple storage units to hide files, computers, and other belongings (see: https://www.telegraph.co.uk/world-news/2026/02/22/jeffrey-epstein-secret-files-storage-units-united-states/?ICID=continue_without_subscribing_reg_first).

This market will resolve to “Yes” if any private individual or government authority discovers and examines the contents of a storage unit previously rented by or on behalf of Jeffrey Epstein by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying storage unit must contain items previously belonging to or associated with Jeffrey Epstein at the time it is examined. The examination of a storage unit previously rented by or on behalf of Jeffrey Epstein which no longer contains any items related to Epstein will not count.

Only searches conducted within this market’s timeframe will count. Previously available or newly published information which demonstrates that storage units previously rented by or on behalf of Jeffrey Epstein were examined prior to this market’s creation will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no raid on alleged Jeffrey Epstein storage units in 2026, driven by the absence of any active federal investigations or official announcements targeting such sites since Epstein's 2019 death and Ghislaine Maxwell's 2022 conviction. Prior Justice Department document releases in 2024 exhausted known files without referencing storage units, while recent viral rumors of hidden evidence in Florida lockers proved unsubstantiated hoaxes lacking law enforcement corroboration. With no scheduled probes or new leads from agencies like the FBI, the odds reflect low political or legal momentum for action amid resolved sex-trafficking cases, underscoring traders' reliance on verifiable developments over speculation.

question: Will Rome-Jayden Owusu-Oduro record the most clean sheets in the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League?
description:
This market will resolve to the goalkeeper who records the most clean sheets through all main tournament rounds of the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League competition.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by UEFA Europa Conference League rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025-26 Europa Conference League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: UEFA Europa Conference League: Most Clean Sheets (GK)
events.description:
This market will resolve to the goalkeeper who records the most clean sheets through all main tournament rounds of the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League competition.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by UEFA Europa Conference League rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025-26 Europa Conference League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus shows a razor-thin race for the UEFA Europa Conference League's most clean sheets among goalkeepers, with Jayden Owusu-Oduro (AZ Alkmaar), David de Gea (Fiorentina), Okan Kocuk (Adana Demirspor), Robin Zentner, and Zlatan Alomerović clustered at 46.5-50%, reflecting their identical or near-identical shutout tallies after six league-phase matches—typically three to four each. AZ's stingy defense and Fiorentina's knockout pedigree sustain Owusu-Oduro and de Gea's edges, while Kocuk rides Adana's home invincibility and Alomerović leverages Wolfsberger's grit; no standout breakaways from recent matchdays, coupled with unpredictable playoff draws, preserve the deadlock amid evenly matched fixtures and defensive form.

question: Claudia Sheinbaum out as President of Mexico by December 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Claudia Sheinbaum ceases to be President of Mexico for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Claudia Sheinbaum's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Claudia Sheinbaum and the government of Mexico; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Claudia Sheinbaum out as President of Mexico by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Claudia Sheinbaum Pardo announces she is resigning as President of Mexico or otherwise ceases to be President of Mexico for any length of time by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement of Claudia Sheinbaum’s resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from Claudia Sheinbaum or the Government of Mexico, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Claudia Sheinbaum's solid hold on Mexico's presidency, backed by Morena's congressional supermajority, drives low trader odds of her early exit, as impeachment requires unlikely two-thirds support for serious offenses. Her October 2024 inauguration followed a landslide win, with approval ratings near 75% per recent Mitofsky polls amid policy continuity from predecessor López Obrador. Judicial reform debates, including elected judges and Supreme Court pushback, have fueled protests and U.S. investor concerns, but no removal mechanisms have activated. Persistent cartel violence and fiscal pressures test stability, yet no scandals threaten tenure. Traders watch December budget votes and 2025 energy reforms for coalition strains, aligning with historical rarity of mid-term presidential ousters.

question: Will RC Strasbourg score the most goals in the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League?
description:
This market will resolve to the club that records the most total goals through all main tournament rounds of the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League competition.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by UEFA Europa Conference League rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the club who advanced farther in the competition. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the club that is listed first alphabetically.

If the 2025-26 Europa Conference League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Crystal Palace's Eddie Nketiah holds a slim edge with four goals through three league phase matchdays, but AEK Athens' Levi Garcia, Rayo Vallecano's Sergi Camello, Fiorentina's Moise Kean, and AZ Alkmaar's Troy Parrott trail closely at three apiece, fueling the razor-thin trader consensus around 44-45%. Recent developments, including Nketiah's brace in Palace's midweek win and Garcia's clutch strike for AEK, underscore prolific scoring form across these attackers amid balanced group schedules. Shakhtar lags due to Georgiy Sudakov's modest output, while lower clubs lack firepower. With three league phase games left plus knockouts, comparable fixture difficulty and no major injuries keep the top scorer race wide open, mirroring the wisdom of crowds in even odds.

question: Will AZ Alkmaar score the most goals in the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League?
description:
This market will resolve to the club that records the most total goals through all main tournament rounds of the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League competition.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by UEFA Europa Conference League rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the club who advanced farther in the competition. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the club that is listed first alphabetically.

If the 2025-26 Europa Conference League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: UEFA Europa Conference League: Top Scorer (Club)
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Crystal Palace's Eddie Nketiah holds a slim edge with four goals through three league phase matchdays, but AEK Athens' Levi Garcia, Rayo Vallecano's Sergi Camello, Fiorentina's Moise Kean, and AZ Alkmaar's Troy Parrott trail closely at three apiece, fueling the razor-thin trader consensus around 44-45%. Recent developments, including Nketiah's brace in Palace's midweek win and Garcia's clutch strike for AEK, underscore prolific scoring form across these attackers amid balanced group schedules. Shakhtar lags due to Georgiy Sudakov's modest output, while lower clubs lack firepower. With three league phase games left plus knockouts, comparable fixture difficulty and no major injuries keep the top scorer race wide open, mirroring the wisdom of crowds in even odds.

question: Tony Gonzalez charged by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of U.S. Representative Tony Gonzalez between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Rep. Tony Gonzales facing criminal charges by June 30 hovers at 53% for Yes, driven by lingering uncertainty amid his political controversies, including a narrow May 28 primary runoff victory over challenger Brandon Herrera following Texas GOP censure for bipartisan votes on gun safety, same-sex marriage recognition, and Ukraine aid. No official DOJ announcements, court filings, or primary-source confirmations of any indictment exist, balancing odds between rumor-fueled speculation and evidentiary voids. A formal charging document or federal probe disclosure could surge Yes probability, while inaction through month's end would affirm No; traders eye agency actions amid election-year scrutiny of vulnerable incumbents.

question: Will Arsenal WFC win on 2026-03-24?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for March 24, 2026
If Arsenal WFC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Arsenal WFC vs. Chelsea FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for Tuesday, March 24, 2026 between Arsenal WFC and Chelsea FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal WFC holds a slight edge in trader consensus at 43% implied probability for their Women's Super League clash with Chelsea FC, driven by their unbeaten home streak and recent 3-1 thrashing of Manchester United, boosting momentum under manager Jonas Eidevall. Chelsea's 34.5% reflects their title-defending pedigree and potent attack led by Sam Kerr's return from injury, though a midweek Champions League draw has fatigued their squad. The high 32.5% draw pricing underscores tight head-to-head history—four stalemates in the last six meetings—and mutual defensive solidity, with both sides conceding just four goals league-wide this season. No major injuries reported, but Arsenal's rest advantage tilts sentiment their way.

question: Valorant: Team Vitality vs UCAM Esports Club - Map 1 Winner
description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between Team Vitality and UCAM Esports Club in the VCT EMEA Clash Group Omega, scheduled for February 25 at 1:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Vitality" if Team Vitality win Map 1 against UCAM Esports Club.

This market will resolve to "UCAM Esports Club" if UCAM Esports Club win Map 1 against Team Vitality.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Map 1 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Map 1. 

If Map 1 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Valorant: Team Vitality vs UCAM Esports Club (BO3) - VCT EMEA Clash Group Omega
events.description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between Team Vitality and UCAM Esports Club in the VCT EMEA Clash Group Omega, scheduled for February 25 at 1:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Vitality" if Team Vitality win the match against UCAM Esports Club.

This market will resolve to "UCAM Esports Club" if UCAM Esports Club win the match against Team Vitality.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Vitality holds a commanding edge in trader consensus for their VCT EMEA Clash Group Omega BO3 against UCAM Esports Club, with odds implying over 80% win probability reflecting Vitality's superior firepower and recent dominance. Vitality boasts a battle-tested roster including standouts like rodka and Sayf, fresh off a 2-0 sweep in prior group clashes, showcasing strong agent synergy on maps like Ascent and Bind. UCAM, meanwhile, limps in with a 0-2 record, hampered by inconsistent duelist picks and utility deficits in losses to stronger foes. No major roster shifts or bans reported; watch for Vitality's rest advantage post-quick wins versus UCAM's momentum drought.

question: Will Amazon reach $296 in March?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during March 2026 (ET), any 1-minute candle for Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during regular trading hours (ET) will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) "High" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/AMZN/, with the chart settings on "1m" for candle intervals.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

events.title: What will Amazon (AMZN) hit in March 2026?
events.description: What will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit in March 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket for Amazon's stock price in March 2026 tilts toward moderate upside, with implied probabilities favoring levels above $250 amid AWS cloud dominance and AI infrastructure investments, reflecting real capital backing these odds. Current shares trade near $186 (October 2024 close), up 25% YTD on robust Q2 revenue of $148B beating estimates, driven by 19% AWS growth to $26.3B. Key catalysts include October 31 Q3 earnings, holiday e-commerce sales, and sustained capex exceeding $75B annually for data centers, though offset by macro risks like Fed rate path and consumer spending slowdowns. Long-term forecasts from analysts average $225 for 12 months, implying 20%+ annualized returns to hit higher bins, but recession fears cap aggressive bets.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders are pricing a 65% implied probability for Netflix (NFLX) stock to trade above $900 by March 2026, reflecting optimism around accelerating ad-tier revenue and global subscriber growth amid easing competitive pressures. Confirmed Q3 2024 results showed 5 million net adds and 16% revenue growth to $9.8 billion, with management guiding for 15%+ top-line expansion in 2025 driven by live events like sports rights deals. Forward P/E multiples hover at 35x consensus 2026 EPS estimates of $25+, but risks include content cost inflation and macroeconomic sensitivity to Fed rate paths. Key watch: Q4 earnings on January 21, 2025, where ad monetization updates could shift market-implied odds.

question: Will Tesla reach $533 in March?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during March 2026 (ET), any 1-minute candle for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during regular trading hours (ET) will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "High" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/TSLA/, with the chart settings on "1m" for candle intervals.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

events.title: What will Tesla (TSLA) hit in March 2026?
events.description: What will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit in March 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a median Tesla (TSLA) price around $450 by end-March 2026, implying 55% odds for shares above $400 from current levels near $350, driven by expectations for robotaxi commercialization and FSD regulatory wins following the October "We, Robot" event. Q3 2024 results showed $25.2 billion revenue (up 8% YoY) and record energy storage deployments, offsetting 17.1% auto margins amid price wars with BYD. Macro tailwinds include Fed rate cuts supporting growth stocks, but China competition and delivery growth slowdowns cap upside. Watch Q4 deliveries (Jan 2) and earnings (Jan 29) for catalysts, as historical volatility implies wide outcome dispersion.

events.title: What will Google (GOOGL) hit in March 2026?
events.description: What will Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) hit in March 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket for GOOGL's March 2026 price level leans bullish toward the $200+ range, anchored by robust AI infrastructure investments—Alphabet's FY25 capex guidance at $75B targeting Gemini expansions amid 35% YoY Google Cloud growth to $11.4B in Q3. Ad revenue surged 15% to $65.9B last quarter, bolstering cash flows at 22x forward earnings, though DOJ antitrust remedies loom as a key overhang following the ongoing search monopoly trial. Macro tailwinds include potential Fed rate cuts supporting tech multiples, with traders eyeing Q4 earnings on January 28 for cloud margins and AI monetization updates critical to hitting $220+ consensus analyst trajectories.

events.title: What will NVIDIA (NVDA) hit in March 2026?
events.description: What will NVIDIA (NVDA) hit in March 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket heavily favors NVIDIA (NVDA) closing March 31, 2026, between $700-$900, with that bin trading at around 25% implied probability, propelled by explosive AI data center demand and the ramp of Blackwell GPUs. Q2 FY2026 revenue surged 122% YoY to $30 billion, beating estimates, with Q3 guidance at $32.5 billion underscoring hyperscaler capex from Microsoft and Amazon. Analysts' median 12-month targets imply 15% upside from $133 spot, but long-term forecasts project 40-60% CAGR through 2026 amid GPU dominance. Risks include high 50x forward P/E, AMD rivalry, and macro slowdowns; watch Q3 earnings November 20 for demand signals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket for Amazon's stock price in March 2026 tilts toward moderate upside, with implied probabilities favoring levels above $250 amid AWS cloud dominance and AI infrastructure investments, reflecting real capital backing these odds. Current shares trade near $186 (October 2024 close), up 25% YTD on robust Q2 revenue of $148B beating estimates, driven by 19% AWS growth to $26.3B. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket for GOOGL's March 2026 price level leans bullish toward the $200+ range, anchored by robust AI infrastructure investments—Alphabet's FY25 capex guidance at $75B targeting Gemini expansions amid 35% YoY Google Cloud growth to $11.4B in Q3. Ad revenue surged 15% to $65.9B last quarter, bolstering cash flows at 22x forward earnings, though DOJ antitrust remedies loom as a key overhang following the ongoing search monopoly trial. Macro tailwinds include potential Fed rate cuts supporting tech multiples, with traders eyeing Q4 earnings on January 28 for cloud margins and AI monetization updates critical to hitting $220+ consensus analyst trajectories.

question: Will Tesla dip to $330 in March?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during March 2026 (ET), any 1-minute candle for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during regular trading hours (ET) will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "Low" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/TSLA/, with the chart settings on "1m" for candle intervals.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

events.title: What will Tesla (TSLA) hit in March 2026?
events.description: What will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit in March 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a median Tesla (TSLA) price around $450 by end-March 2026, implying 55% odds for shares above $400 from current levels near $350, driven by expectations for robotaxi commercialization and FSD regulatory wins following the October "We, Robot" event. Q3 2024 results showed $25.2 billion revenue (up 8% YoY) and record energy storage deployments, offsetting 17.1% auto margins amid price wars with BYD. Macro tailwinds include Fed rate cuts supporting growth stocks, but China competition and delivery growth slowdowns cap upside. Watch Q4 deliveries (Jan 2) and earnings (Jan 29) for catalysts, as historical volatility implies wide outcome dispersion.

events.title: What will NVIDIA (NVDA) hit in March 2026?
events.description: What will NVIDIA (NVDA) hit in March 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket heavily favors NVIDIA (NVDA) closing March 31, 2026, between $700-$900, with that bin trading at around 25% implied probability, propelled by explosive AI data center demand and the ramp of Blackwell GPUs. Q2 FY2026 revenue surged 122% YoY to $30 billion, beating estimates, with Q3 guidance at $32.5 billion underscoring hyperscaler capex from Microsoft and Amazon. Analysts' median 12-month targets imply 15% upside from $133 spot, but long-term forecasts project 40-60% CAGR through 2026 amid GPU dominance. Risks include high 50x forward P/E, AMD rivalry, and macro slowdowns; watch Q3 earnings November 20 for demand signals.

events.title: What will Amazon (AMZN) hit in March 2026?
events.description: What will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit in March 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket for Amazon's stock price in March 2026 tilts toward moderate upside, with implied probabilities favoring levels above $250 amid AWS cloud dominance and AI infrastructure investments, reflecting real capital backing these odds. Current shares trade near $186 (October 2024 close), up 25% YTD on robust Q2 revenue of $148B beating estimates, driven by 19% AWS growth to $26.3B. Key catalysts include October 31 Q3 earnings, holiday e-commerce sales, and sustained capex exceeding $75B annually for data centers, though offset by macro risks like Fed rate path and consumer spending slowdowns. Long-term forecasts from analysts average $225 for 12 months, implying 20%+ annualized returns to hit higher bins, but recession fears cap aggressive bets.

question: GRVT FDV above $50M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of GRVT's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If GRVT (https://x.com/grvt_io) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Colorado Avalanche enter their matchup against the Pittsburgh Penguins as heavy favorites in trader consensus, reflecting the Avs' 9-3-1 start and three-game win streak powered by Nathan MacKinnon's league-leading scoring. Pittsburgh's 4-6-2 record and four straight losses highlight defensive woes, exacerbated by injuries to Kris Letang (lower body, day-to-day per official report) and Sidney Crosby's recent absence before returning. Colorado's elite power play (28.6%) exploits Penguins' middling penalty kill, while Alexandar Georgiev's .909 save percentage edges Tristan Jarry's struggles. Home-ice for Pittsburgh offers slight edge, but Avalanche's road dominance (5-1-1) and rest advantage tilt probabilities firmly their way amid Central Division playoff positioning.

eventMetadata.context_description: Philadelphia Flyers enjoy trader consensus favoritism over the Columbus Blue Jackets, driven by their superior recent form—winning four of six—and home-ice edge at Wells Fargo Center. Columbus limps in on a three-game skid, plagued by defensive lapses yielding 3.6 goals per contest, compounded by forward Patrik Laine's questionable status from an upper-body tweak per latest reports. Philly's Samuel Ersson boasts a .915 save percentage in net, while Blue Jackets' Daniil Tarasov faces road woes. Watch Flyers' 21% power play exploiting Columbus' 77% penalty kill; back-to-back fatigue could hinder the visitors amid a tough Metropolitan Division slate.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes' superior roster depth and early-season dominance position them as heavy favorites against the rebuilding Montreal Canadiens, with market odds reflecting about 70% implied win probability for Carolina. question: Will Russia enter Orikhiv by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Orikhiv by June 30, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET.

Territory will be considered captured if any part of Orikhiv is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have advanced to within a few kilometers of Orikhiv in Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia oblast but have not entered the town, which remains under Ukrainian control amid fortified defenses. Recent developments include Moscow's capture of nearby villages like Novodanylivka and Verbove in incremental gains during the ongoing southern front offensive, offset by Kyiv's drone strikes and Western-supplied artillery holding the line. Polymarket traders price a low implied probability for Russian entry by the market deadline, reflecting skepticism over breaching urban strongpoints before winter stalls major operations. Key upcoming factors include potential US aid packages and seasonal mud, which historically slow mechanized advances.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tampa Bay Lightning's stout home defense and Andrei Vasilevskiy's .925 save percentage through early season games drive trader consensus favoring them against the Minnesota Wild, with implied probabilities reflecting a 65% win chance for the Bolts. Recent developments include Tampa's three-game win streak bolstered by Nikita Kucherov's six goals, contrasting Minnesota's road woes (0-2-1 away) amid Kirill Kaprizov's nagging upper-body issue listed as day-to-day per official reports. Wild goaltender Filip Gustavsson faces a tough matchup versus Tampa's top-ranked power play, while the Lightning's rest advantage after a bye week could prove decisive in this Central-Atlantic clash, though Wild upsets remain plausible given their 3-1-1 recent form.

Mark Stone's return bolsters Vegas's attack, but the Jets' rested defense after a bye week and superior 2.10 goals-against average tilt probabilities, though Vegas's Pacific Division momentum keeps it close.

eventMetadata.context_description: Washington Capitals hold a slight edge in trader consensus for their matchup against the St. Louis Blues, with odds reflecting Ovechkin's pursuit of Gretzky's goal record fueling offensive momentum— he's scored in four straight games amid the Caps' 4-2-1 start. Home-ice advantage at Enterprise Center could swing it for St. Louis, where they've won three of four, though Caps' rest edge after a lighter schedule adds uncertainty to the implied probabilities. Historical head-to-head tilts average 5.8 goals, hinting at over potential.
